rationale.summary:
Decision: Roll Back
Critical anomalies: 4, High: 1
Criteria passed: 0/4
Sentiment score: 30.0/100
Key drivers: crash rate 4.2x baseline, payment failures, API latency 3x baseline, accelerating support tickets and cancellations.

risk_register.risk: Crash rate 4.2x above baseline (Android WebView known issue)
risk_register.mitigation: Hotfix for Android WebView crash; feature-flag disable for affected devices

risk_register.risk: Payment success rate declining — revenue leakage risk
risk_register.mitigation: Revert payment pre-fill integration; patch legacy token handling

risk_register.risk: API latency p95 at 640ms (3x baseline) — auto-scaling lag unresolved
risk_register.mitigation: Pre-warm recommend-svc replicas; lower auto-scale trigger to 50% CPU

risk_register.risk: Daily cancellations 3.5x baseline — retention crisis if unaddressed
risk_register.mitigation: Pause full rollout; offer affected users service credit

action_plan_24_48h.action: Disable SmartRecommend v2 for Android 10 devices via feature flag
Revert payment pre-fill integration; restore legacy token compatibility
Pre-warm recommend-svc replicas; lower auto-scale CPU trigger to 50%
Publish status page update acknowledging performance degradation
Deploy self-serve FAQ for top 3 support ticket categories
Ship Android WebView crash hotfix and validate on staging
Update recommendation model with current catalogue (fix out-of-stock SKUs)
War room check-in: review all metrics post-fixes before resuming rollout

internal: Immediate Slack alert to #engineering-oncall: crash rate and payment failures require P1 response
Executive summary to VP Product and CTO within 1 hour
Daily war room standup at 09:00 until metrics stabilise
external: Status page update: 'We are aware of performance issues affecting some users and are actively working on a fix'
Email to affected users (payment failures) within 4 hours with apology and resolution ETA
Hold all promotional campaigns for SmartRecommend v2 until Proceed decision
Community forum post acknowledging crash reports with workaround instructions

agent_outputs.data_analyst:

  - support_ticket_volume: +297.7% vs baseline [critical]
  - daily_cancellations: +250.0% vs baseline [critical]
  - api_latency_p95_ms: +190.9% vs baseline [critical]
  - feature_adoption_funnel_pct: -29.2% vs baseline [high]
  - dau_wau_ratio: -23.8% vs baseline [medium]
  - d7_retention_pct: -22.7% vs baseline [medium]
  - activation_conversion_pct: -21.3% vs baseline [medium]
  - d1_retention_pct: -19.4% vs baseline [medium]
Critical anomalies: 4, High: 1
agent_outputs.pm:
Go/No-Go Assessment: FAIL
  ✗ crash_rate_below_1pct
  ✗ payment_success_above_97pct
  ✗ no_critical_anomalies
  ✗ support_tickets_stable
Critical issues: 4, High issues: 1
Recommendation: Pause rollout pending crash rate and payment fixes.
agent_outputs.marketing:
Sentiment score: 30.0/100 (negative-leaning)
Negative: 21, Positive: 7, Neutral: 7
Top negative themes: crash / stability (8), payment failures (6), performance / latency (3)
Comms posture: Proactive acknowledgement required. Do NOT promote feature externally.
Internal: Escalate to engineering immediately. External: Status page update + email to affected users.
agent_outputs.risk:
Risk assessment: ROLL BACK or PAUSE strongly recommended.
4 risks identified:
  [High/High] Crash rate 4.2x above baseline (Android WebView known issue)
  [High/Critical] Payment success rate declining — revenue leakage risk
  [High/High] API latency p95 at 640ms (3x baseline) — auto-scaling lag unresolved
  [Medium/High] Daily cancellations 3.5x baseline — retention crisis if unaddressed
Key assumption challenged: 'Canary looked fine' — canary was only 10% traffic; issues emerged at scale.
